title: podman build and process substitution for Containerfile
body:
Recent changes in buildah create a overlay mount https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/7c58fc17f75731e839367cadcd16a50249ed6a3c on the context dir.

When using something like `podman build -f <(<<<"FROM scratch")` the shell will expand that to `/dev/fd/<NUM>` as path and when no context directory is given podman defaults to the directory of the Containerfile path. So in this case the context dir ends up being `/dev/fd`, and then when buildah tries to mount an overlay using that ass lower the kernel does not like that and fail with EINVAL.

Arguably using /dev/fd as context dir is nonsensical to begin with and the buildah fix is valid so we should figure out how we want to handle that because it break some test cases of buildah that we like to run here https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/72a0a1179ea0027de7774dd60e258da33adaf960

The reason this works with buildah is because it defaults to the cwd instead.

So how should podman deal with that? Should we special case the /dev/fd case and use an empty tmp context dir instead or use the cwd as well in that case? The issue with the cwd is that on podman-remote the full dir will be copied which would be bad as most people run the command from the home dir I would guess.

comments.body:
cc @nalind @containers/podman-maintainers WDYT?

I guess the other option is do not allow that and keep erroring? If we do that we likely need a saner error though, the mount failure will not be obvious to end users
```
Error: mounting an overlay over build context directory: creating overlay scaffolding for build context directory: mount overlay:/var/tmp/buildah-context-3179859923/overlay/2646863032/merge, data: lowerdir=/dev/fd,upperdir=/var/tmp/buildah-context-3179859923/overlay/2646863032/upper,workdir=/var/tmp/buildah-context-3179859923/overlay/2646863032/work,context="system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c271,c360",userxattr: invalid argument
```

I ran into this in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27993#discussion_r2754138912.

> The issue with the cwd is that on podman-remote the full dir will be copied which would be bad as most people run the command from the home dir I would guess.

Is the concern that we're unnecessarily exposing client dir paths? Because ideally I would expect podman to mirror buildah's behavior and use cwd if the context directory is not specified. Even if we handle /dev/fd case, the difference in behaviour is mildly annoying.

Lastly, there's docker that has the context directory a requirement, but I guess that would be too big of a breaking change and doesn't add too much value over setting cwd.

> Is the concern that we're unnecessarily exposing client dir paths? Because ideally I would expect podman to mirror buildah's behavior and use cwd if the context directory is not specified. Even if we handle /dev/fd case, the difference in behaviour is mildly annoying.

Well that is one of them, but the more practical one is that your home dir might be quite large which would make the copying of the context really slow and users would not understand why nothing happens. And also you may run our of space on the server side, for things like podman machine that would be a huge issue.

You're right, I didn't think of that at first. I believe, then, that erroring out seems to be the only way forward. Though we should try to improve the error message lest it leave users confused. It took me a while to actually figure this out when I encountered this issue.

Also, would it be helpful to bring buildah in line with podman (the other way round is impossible as we saw) ie defaulting to the current Containerfile directory as default? Or perhaps making it a required argument as Docker does? Unsure still if there's a reason buildah does it the way it does currently.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
whats the downside of an empty context dir then ?

> whats the downside of an empty context dir then ?

I don't think there is a downside in the case of the containerfile being `/dev/fd/...`, but I think if you use it on a regular dir like `podman build -f somedir/Containefile` then no longer defaulting to `somedir` will almost certainly break people.

So do we like to add such a special case or are we fine breaking some people who do not explicitly give the context dir arg? We still have a chance with podman 6.

Personally I am not a fan of special cases but I think it this case here it might make sense and is likely the best thing to do to keep everything working without breaking anybody,

What's the consensus here? The options in this issue, if I'm understanding it correctly are

if it's a `/dev/fd/...`,
1. use the cwd instead (buildah compatable behavior, could end up with large contexts)
2.  use a temp context dir
3. Just display a prettier error message

cc @Luap99 @nalind
I would vote for number 2, but I don't have strong opinions.
